'''Robert Worrincy''' (born 9 July 1985) is an English former [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Hunslet RLFC]] in [[RFL League 1]].

He previously played in the [[Super League]] for the [[Castleford Tigers]], and in the second tier for the [[Sheffield Eagles]] over three separate spells, two separate spells at [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in the second tier, as well as the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship]]. Worrincy also spent time as a [[rugby union]] footballer, playing a season as a [[Rugby union positions#14. & 11. Wing|winger]] for [[Doncaster R.F.C.|Doncaster]] in the second tier of English rugby union.

==Background==
Worrincy was born in [[Leeds]], [[West Yorkshire]], England. He is of Anglo-Nigerian-Welsh heritage. He began playing rugby league in the [[London]] area, and won the [[BARLA]] Youth Player of the Year Award in 2003.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rob's the tops |url=https://www.newsshopper.co.uk/sport/othersport/448536.robs-the-tops/ |website=News Shopper |access-date=8 July 2022 |date=12 January 2004}}</ref>

He is the elder brother of the rugby league player; [[Michael Worrincy]].

==Career==
In 2004, he made his profession debut for [[Castleford Tigers]] in the [[Super League]]. He went on to have multiple spells with the [[Sheffield Eagles]] and [[Halifax R.L.F.C.|Halifax]] in the [[Championship (rugby league)|Kingstone Press Championship]].

In March 2014, Worrincy was suspended for six months after breaching the [[Rugby Football League]]'s rules on betting.<ref>{{cite web |title=Rob Worrincy: Halifax winger gets six-month ban for betting breach |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/26785035 |website=BBC Sport |access-date=8 July 2022 |date=28 March 2014}}</ref> The ban was extended to nine months following a failed appeal.<ref>{{cite web |title=Worrincy ban extended |url=https://www.itv.com/news/calendar/update/2014-07-24/worrincy-ban-extended/ |publisher=ITV |access-date=8 July 2022 |date=24 July 2014}}</ref>

In November 2017 he signed to play for Dewsbury Rams.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.dewsburyreporter.co.uk/sport/more-sport/rams-swoop-in-for-winger-worrincy-1-8862027|title=Rams swoop in for winger Worrincy|publisher=Dewsbury Reporter|date=16 November 2017|accessdate=19 December 2017}}</ref>

In 2021, while at Sheffield, Worrincy announced his retirement from the game.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.sheffieldeagles.com/Eagles-News/article/championship-legend-worrincy-to-retire-/|title=Championship legend Worrincy to retire at end of season|publisher=Sheffield Eagles|date=1 September 2021|accessdate=19 September 2021}}</ref> He scored a try in the last minute of his final game in 78-10 loss at [[Featherstone Rovers]].

In May 2022, Worrincy came out of retirement and signed for [[Rochdale Hornets]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Hornets sign former Halifax and Sheffield winger Rob Worrincy |url=https://www.rochdaleonline.co.uk/news-features/4/sport-news/145942/hornets-sign-former-halifax-and-sheffield-winger-rob-worrincy |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220516023436/https://www.rochdaleonline.co.uk/news-features/4/sport-news/145942/hornets-sign-former-halifax-and-sheffield-winger-rob-worrincy |url-status=usurped |archive-date=16 May 2022 |publisher=Rochdale Online |access-date=8 July 2022 |date=13 May 2022}}</ref>

On 18 August 2023 it was announced that he had received a three-year doping ban by UKAD.<ref>{{cite web |title=UKAD Statement - Rob Worrincy|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/62048/ukad-statement-%7C-rob-worrincy|website=Rugby Leaguer|accessdate=18 Aug 2023 |date=18 Aug 2023}}</ref>
